data.title: Transocean President/COO Adamson to become President/CEO, succeeding Thigpen
data.description:
Transocean (NYSE:RIG) said Tuesday that President and COO Keelan Adamson will become President and CEO following a transition period, which is expected to conclude during this year's Q2, succeeding Jeremy Thigpen, who has led the company as CEO since 2015.

Thigpen is expected to be appointed as Executive Chair, with current Chair Chad Deaton transitioning to the role of Lead Independent Director.

Adamson joined Transocean (NYSE:RIG) in 1995 and has served as President and COO since February 2022; previously, he was the company's Executive VP and COO during 2018-22, Senior VP of Operations in 2017-18, and Senior VP of Operations Integrity and HSE during 2015-17.

Thigpen joined Transocean (RIG) as the company's CEO in April 2015, and served as President until February 2022; he had served 18 years at National Oilweel Varco, where he became Senior VP and CFO as well as the company's President of Downhole and Pumping Solutions business and President of its Downhole Tools group.

data.title: Hillman Solutions misses Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Hillman Solutions press release (NASDAQ:HLMN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $349.6M (+0.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.85M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA totaled $56.3 million compared to $54.4 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities totaled $183.3 million compared to $238.0 million in the prior year.
  • Free Cash Flow totaled $98.1 million compared to $172.3 million in the prior year.

  • Full Year 2025 Guidance

    Hillman has provided the following guidance based on its current view of the market and its performance expectations during the fifty-two weeks ended December 27, 2025.

    Full Year 2025 Guidance
    Net Sales $1.495 to $1.575 billion vs. $1.54B consensus
    Adjusted EBITDA1 $255 to $275 million
    Free Cash Flow1 $90 to $110 million

data.title: Adecoagro stock rises on receipt of unsolicited proposal from Tether
data.description:
Adecoagro (NYSE:AGRO) shares jumped 9% premarket on Tuesday after it confirmed receipt of an unsolicited non-binding proposal from Tether Investments to acquire outstanding common shares of the company at $12.41 per share through a tender offer.

Tether is already a shareholder of the agro-industrial company, holding around 19.4% of its outstanding common shares according to its last 13D filing dated November 14, 2024.

A potential deal would result in Tether collectively holding 51% of the outstanding common shares of Adecoagro (NYSE:AGRO).

Adecoagro board of directors held a meeting on February 16, 2025 to discuss the terms and conditions of the proposal and decided to engage legal and financial advisors to further assist in its evaluation of the proposal.

data.title: VSE Corp. to divest fleet segment for up to $230M
data.description:
VSE Corporation (NASDAQ:VSEC) has entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Fleet business segment, Wheeler Fleet Solutions, to One Equity Partners for up to $230M in total consideration.

The consideration comprises a $140M cash payment at closing, a $25M seller note and up to $65M in additional contingent earnout consideration.

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.

"The sale of our Fleet business is the final step in our strategic portfolio transformation, further simplifying and focusing our company, and strengthening our global leadership position as an aviation aftermarket parts and services provider," said John Cuomo, president and CEO of VSE Corporation.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Nine in 10 fund managers say U.S. stocks overvalued, most since 2001
data.description:
The most money managers in more than 20 years see U.S. stocks as overpriced, according to the latest BofA survey.

The February Global Fund Manager Survey of 205 panelists with $482B in assets under management, shows that 89% of respondents see U.S. stocks (NYSEARCA:SPY) (QQQ) (DIA) as overvalued, the most since at least April 2001.

Still, strategist Michael Hartnett notes that while the level is at a two-decade high, for the 2020s an average of 81% of fund managers have seen U.S. stocks as overvalued.

In addition, the U.S. exceptionalism trade looks to have peaked, becoming slightly less crowded in February, Hartnett said

The trade "has been characterized by a combo of strong US dollar (DXY) and an equity bull market led by the 'Magnificent 7' group of stocks," he said.

This month, long U.S. exceptionalism "was viewed as the most crowded trade by 73% of FMS investors, a decline from the three-year high of 80% reached in January."

Money managers are also putting cash to work, with cash levels of 3.5% a 15-year low.

Equity investors are rotating to bond-sensitive assets and Europe as global recession expectations fade.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Medtronic FQ3 earnings beat estimates, revenue misses; reaffirms FY25 outlook
Russia denies reports of OPEC+ oil supply hike delay
data.description:
It's not easy to decide oil strategy in the current climate. Fresh reports over the weekend suggested that OPEC+ delegates were considering a delay in restoring output, before they were denied by major producer Russia. While OPEC+ members meet monthly to set quotas for crude production, there will be more on the table next time around with oil (CL1:COM) recently giving up all its gains seen since the beginning of the year.

Crude reality: A promise by President Trump to "drill, baby, drill" will continue to see America produce more crude than any country on record, while the threat of tariffs on global growth has also weighed on crude. It's despite new sanctions on nations like Iran and commitments by the U.S. to cut the oil exports of the Islamic Republic. Meanwhile, many big countries like China and India are still getting their crude from shadow fleets, and members within OPEC+ have pumped more than their official quotas in a test that can challenge the cartel's unity.

OPEC+ is currently cutting 5.85M barrels per day, which is about 5.7% of global supply, and in December, the group extended its latest cuts through the first quarter of 2025. It marked the latest delay due to weaker demand and increasing outside supply, but some members are getting antsy, like the United Arab Emirates. It feels that it has done more of the heavy lifting amid a campaign for higher quotas, and any new delays could be met with resistance.

SA commentary: "There's a lot of spare capacity that can be deployed into this market," TD Wealth writes in Will Trump's Calls For OPEC To Lower Oil Prices Work? "I think OPEC doesn't really want to do that because they know a 10%, let's say, increase in supply would cause more than a percent reduction in price. And that would not necessarily be accretive for the revenue side for them. So, I think they're going to play it by ear, see how things go, and keep the market as balanced as they possibly can."

data.title: Valmont raises dividend by 13% to $0.68/share; announces $700M stock buyback authorization
data.description:
• Valmont (NYSE:VMI) declares $0.68/share quarterly dividend, 13.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.60.
  • Forward yield 0.83%
  • Payable April 15; for shareholders of record March 28; ex-div March 28.
  • The company anticipates increasing its dividend annually in the first quarter of each year, at a pace aligned with expected longer-term earnings growth. The board of directors has also approved a new $700M share repurchase authorization, representing approximately 10% of the company’s current market capitalization.
  • See VMI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: The GameStop plot thickens as the company looks to sell assets in France and Canada
data.description:
GameStop Corp. (NYSE:GME) announced on Tuesday that it intends to pursue a sale of its operations in France and Canada. The retailer said the decision was part of its evaluation of its international assets.

The development is interesting after GameStop (NYSE:GME) rallied last week amid buzz over a possible entry back into the crypto sector. CNBC reported that the retailer is eyeing investments in alternative asset classes, including crypto, and especially bitcoin.

Sources indicated that GameStop (GME) is still figuring out if the investment is sensible for the business. The report caught the attention of investors because GME had $4.6 billion in cash holdings at the end of Q3, compared to its current market cap of $12.1 billion.

Investors may remember that GameStop (GME) has a mixed history with cryptocurrency and blockchain initiatives in general. The company launched a self-custodial cryptocurrency wallet in 2022 that allowed customers to manage digital assets, including cryptocurrencies and non-fungible tokens. The move was part of GameStop's (GME) broader strategy to expand into the digital asset space.

Shares of GameStop (GME) moved 1.4% higher in premarket action on Tuesday.
